candidates.hook: People don't buy what you do, they buy why you do it.
candidates.why: Central thesis repeated for emphasis, ties to belief-driven behavior.

candidates.hook: 250,000 people showed up for Dr. King's speech without invitations.
candidates.why: Powerful example of belief-driven mass action vs. product-centric thinking.
Introduces the core concept of the golden circle, framing the talk's thesis.

candidates.hook: TiVo is a commercial failure despite being the best product.
candidates.why: Contrast between product quality and market failure to highlight 'why' importance.

candidates.hook: The goal is not just to sell people who need what you have
candidates.why: Emphasizes the shift from transactional to belief-driven relationships, with a powerful closing line.
